title: salix berberifolia subsp. kimurana
intro: Salix berberifolia subsp. kimurana, also known as Japanese willow or Kimura's willow, is a subspecies of willow native to Japan. It is a shrub or small tree with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Salix berberifolia subsp. kimurana include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make traditional medicines and supplements.
